name: Silence
summary: A negotiation technique that uses deliberate pauses to create thinking space and encourage the other side to reveal more instead of reacting too quickly.
whenToUseIt: Use silence after a buyer makes a request, reacts to a proposal, or needs to process a trade so you do not negotiate against yourself.
steps: Make your point or ask your question clearly.
Pause long enough for the buyer to respond fully instead of rescuing the silence.
Use what they reveal in the pause to guide the next move.
example: After laying out a revised commercial offer, the rep stays quiet long enough for procurement to reveal that payment timing matters more than the headline discount.
